{{Quote|The rallies have recently been decriminalized on Dahvil, so the crowd of spectators present at the staging area is twice as large as it was for the previous promenade.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Dahvil''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Rayter sector/Legends|Rayter sector]]. A member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], it was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Jake Sirrom]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Wing Guard]] of the planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Dahvil served as the starting point of the [[Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade]], a popular [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] racing route. The [[Racer/Legends|racer]] and undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operative [[Siro Simito]] once participated in the race, and at the event's staging area on Dahvil, he picked up from his fellow Rebel agent [[Kaini]] a data module for secret delivery to the celestial body [[Fait d'Fait]].

==Description==
Dahvil was a terrestrial<ref name="CRO" /> [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Facebook" /> located in the [[Dahvil system/Legends|Dahvil system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Rayter sector/Legends|Rayter sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
{{Quote|As usual, Kaini has done a good job, and no one in the crowd realizes that it has all been an act. I know that the data module I am scheduled to deliver is hidden in the casing of the lens, so I pick it up and put it in my pouch. Then I hold my axe up high over my head and the crowd cheers again.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
Dahvil was represented in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Gem Sirrom/Legends|Gem Sirrom]].<ref name="Facebook" /> Gem's [[Gender/Legends|son]] [[Jake Sirrom]] [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from that world. After<ref name="RoguesGallery">{{InsiderCite|148|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: The Cloud City Wing Guards}}</ref> the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial successor]] to the Senate was [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|dissolved]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Gem took Jake to<ref name="RoguesGallery" /> the Outer Rim planet<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] in order to start a new life. There, the latter eventually became a member of the [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Bespin Wing Guard]] [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security force]].<ref name="RoguesGallery" />

[[File:Siro_Simito.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The racing pilot Siro Simito picked up a data module on Dahvil for a secret Rebel Alliance delivery offworld.]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Dahvil served as the starting point of the popular<ref name="CRO" /> [[Standard year/Legends|annual]]<ref name="CSWE Fodro">{{CSWECite|I|284|Fodro}}</ref> [[Star Rally]] [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] racing route known as the [[Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade]]. At one point, not long after the Star Rally races were decriminalized on Dahvil, the [[Racer/Legends|racer]] and undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operative [[Siro Simito]] was tasked by the Alliance to deliver a data module from the celestial body to a contact on<ref name="CRO" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="EAOC" /> astronomical object [[Fait d'Fait]] while participating in the race.<ref name="CRO" />

Similarly to other such missions, the Alliance arranged for the agent [[Kaini]] to hand the data module to Simito. On Dahvil, the former agent posed as a [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] from<ref name="CRO" /> the Mid Rim<ref name="EAOC" /> world<ref name="CSWE Dorlass">{{CSWECite|II|419|Ord Dorlass}}</ref> [[Ord Dorlass]]. As part of the Rebels' pre-arranged act, while Simito was walking through the amassed crowd at the race's staging area, Kaini and her [[Cam droid/Legends|cam droid]], which had the data module hidden in the casing of its supertelephoto lens, approached the racer. The supposed reporter pretended to interview Simito about his feud with the fellow racer [[Seeg]]. Simito—acting like he was lashing out in annoyance at the repeated mentioning of his rival—used his [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]] to slice off the cam [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] lens, and the racer picked it up after Kaini and the droid had fled the scene.<ref name="CRO" />

Subsequently Simito and the other pilots departed from Dahvil and traveled toward the first stopping point of the race, Ord Dorlass. At some point by the [[Time/Legends|time]] that shortly followed the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Rebel Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] included Simito's report, which mentioned the racer's visit to Dahvil, in a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives.<ref name="CRO" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|I've got relatively few fans on Dahvil, so I was able to walk through the crowd with little incident ''[…]'' The crowd around me cheers. Even though they depend on the holovids for news about the rallies, they can't stand the reporters.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
[[File:Jake_Sirrom.png|thumb|right|200px|Dahvil was the homeworld of the Bespin Wing Guard Jake Sirrom.]]
Dahvil was the homeworld of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] Jake Sirrom.<ref name="RoguesGallery" /> Although the Star Rally races were illegal on Dahvil during the Galactic Civil War, spectators nevertheless attended the start of one such race there. At the next race following their decriminalization, however, the crowd was twice as large. Despite relying on holovids for keeping themselves informed about the racing events, the spectators on Dahvil had a dislike for the holovid reporters and, at one point, cheered the pilot Siro Simito after he had what appeared to be an aggressive outburst at a reporter. Simito nevertheless had a relatively small number of fans on the celestial body.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dahvil was created by [[Chuck Truett]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Dahvil [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square J-19.<ref name="TEA Dahvil" />
